verdict_reasoning: DSG3 is a single-pass transmembrane cadherin with multiple extracellular cadherin-repeat domains. It is a constitutive component of desmosomes at the plasma membrane, mediating homophilic cell-cell adhesion between epithelial and myocardial cells. Its surface accessibility is confirmed by pemphigus vulgaris autoimmunity: IgG autoantibodies bind the extracellular domain and disrupt adhesion, proving the protein body is exposed on the outer face. Stably anchored via its transmembrane domain.
